1.description: Idiosyncratic risk in MBS performance. This figure plots three-month moving averages of the 5th, 25th, 50th, 75th, and 95thpercentiles of MBS hedge fund returns to illustrate the idiosyncratic risk in MBS performance. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Performance dispersion and return dynamics of MBS funds. The shaded areas represent the U.S. financial crisis. The sample excludes multiasset funds. We plot 12-month moving average of XS Vol, TS Vol, and Value-weighted returns (in % per month).

3.description: Model comparative statics over variation in total risk. Blue lines plot partial equilibrium comparative statics; red lines plot general equilibrium comparative statics. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Impulse response to an unanticipated volatility shock. This figure plots the transition dynamics for an unanticipated shock that doublesσν$\sigma _\nu$10 quarters. The nine panels plot effective volatility, alpha, participation, the equally weighted Sharpe ratio, realized returns, total wealth, leverage, dispersion in returns, and the share of wealth of the top 10% of investors, respectively.

5.description: Impulse response to wealth shock. This figure plots the transition dynamics for an unanticipated shock that reduces incumbent wealth by 20%. The nine panels plot effective volatility, alpha, participation, the equally weighted Sharpe ratio, realized returns, total wealth, leverage, dispersion in returns, and the share of wealth of the top 10% of investors, respectively.

6.description: Impulse response to an unanticipated volatility shock in the alternative model with heterogeneous risk aversion. This figure plots the transition dynamics for an unanticipated shock thatσν$\sigma _\nu$increases by 20% for 10 quarters. The nine panels plot effective volatility, alpha, participation, the equally weighted Sharpe ratio, realized returns, total wealth, leverage, dispersion in returns, and the share of wealth of the top 10% of investors, respectively.

7.description: Impulse response to an unanticipated volatility shock in the alternative model with heterogeneous maintenance participation costs. This figure plots the transition dynamics for an unanticipated shock thatσν$\sigma _\nu$increases by 20% for 10 quarters. The nine panels plot effective volatility, alpha, participation, the equally weighted Sharpe ratio, realized returns, total wealth, leverage, dispersion in returns, and the share of wealth of the top 10% of investors, respectively.
